{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == - '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.

=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=draft}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|12=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
<!--Kai Kokuzo-->
