=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft|12=draft}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国|12=draft|12=draft}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=draft|12=draft}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=draft|12=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft|12=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=draft|12=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=draft|12=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = <!-- Crest image --> | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft|12=draft}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国|12=draft|12=draft}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=draft|12=draft}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=draft|12=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft|12=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=draft|12=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=draft|12=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
<!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo-->
<!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo-->
<!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo-->

=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=draft|12=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft|12=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=draft|12=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=draft|12=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
<!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo--> <!--Kai Kokuzo-->

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 甲斐国造 |家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[甲斐氏]] |家祖 = [[塩海宿禰]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神|天神]]）または[[皇別]] |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = [[甲斐国]] |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = [[壬生氏]]、[[三枝氏]]（[[武家]]）<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>、（[[穂国造]]、[[浜名県主]]） }} '''甲斐国造'''（かいのくにのみやつこ・かいこくぞう）は、[[甲斐国]]を支配した[[国造]]。 __TOC__{{-}}
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」、『諸系譜』<ref>[[鈴木真年]]「東国諸国造」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref><ref name="nakata">[[中田憲信]]「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>に'''甲斐国造'''とある。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』では若倭根子日子大毘毘命（[[開化天皇]]）が[[和邇氏|丸邇臣]]の祖の日子国意祁都命の妹の[[意祁都比売命]]を娶って生まれた'''[[彦坐王|日子坐王]]'''で、日子坐王が春日之建国勝戸売の女の沙本之大闇見戸売を娶って産んだ子が[[狭穂彦王|沙本毘子王]]であり、この沙本毘子王を甲斐国造と[[日下部氏|日下部連]]の祖としている。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」では、[[景行天皇]]の御代に狭穂彦王の3世の孫・'''臣知津彦公'''の子である4世孫の'''[[塩海宿禰|塩海足尼]]'''が甲斐国造に任じられたと記している。
=== 氏族 === '''[[甲斐氏]]'''（かいうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[君 (カバネ)|君]]）で、[[皇別]]出自については異論がある（[[#考証|後述]]）。
=== 考証 === ==== 国造の氏姓 ==== [[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]には、現地の在地首長が支配していた農民や職業集団が「部」の単位で編制され、在地豪族が掌握する部を中央の諸豪族や伴造らが統括し貢物や労役などを奉仕させる[[部民制]]が成立し、[[ヤマト王権]]が地方部民統括のため地方豪族に対して任命した世襲地位である国造制も整えられたとする説がある。
[[甲斐国]]では『[[正倉院宝物]]』の調庸白あしぎぬ金青袋の墨書銘や[[笛吹市]]一宮町に所在する大原遺跡から出土した[[墨書土器]]から、[[山梨郡]]には王族に従属する名代・子代系の部である[[日下部]]が置かれていたことが確認されている。日下部は東海道や東山道などに広く分布するもので、氏姓に関して[[関晃]]は『古事記』に同祖と記される日下部とし、[[姓]]（かばね）は地方豪族や地方国造にも多く、甲斐国における他の部民管理氏族と共有する[[直 (姓)|直]]であったと推定しており、他の部民管理氏族とは同族関係であったと考えていて、これが通説的な位置づけである。一方、[[鈴木正信]]は二条大路出土木簡に「左大舎人甲斐□」とあることや、他の国造の氏姓が管轄国の地名を負うことから、「甲斐直」を推定している。しかし、両者の説は共に現存史料からは確認できない。
==== 他地域との関係性 ==== 考古学的にも4世紀に築造された[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]の墳丘型式や、同古墳から[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土するなど、畿内王権との強い影響が見られる。
== 本拠 == 中心は塩海宿禰以降は一貫して北部の'''[[山梨郡]]'''にあり、なかでも考古遺跡や主要神社・祭祀などから見て、[[笛吹市]]の旧春日居町あたりから[[甲府市]]東部にかけての地域ではないかとみられる<ref name="houga2" />。
=== 支配領域 === [[甲斐国]]に他の国造が設置されていないため、甲斐国全域を支配していたと考えられる。
== 氏神 == [[甲斐国]][[一宮]]である'''[[浅間神社 (笛吹市)|浅間神社]]'''。
=== 関連神社 === *[[玉諸神社]]（たまもろじんじゃ） *:[[甲府市]]に鎮座する式内論社で甲斐国三宮。国造二代の速彦宿禰が祭祀したとされる。旧鎮座地を御室山といい、社家は磯部氏を称した。 *[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]（みわじんじゃ） *:[[笛吹市]]に鎮座する式内論社で甲斐国二宮。[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に国造の塩見足尼を祭主として祀ったことが起源。 *[[甲斐奈神社]]（かいなじんじゃ） *:式内論社が三社存在する。国造の祖・土本毘古王が祭祀を始めたとされる。 *[[佐久神社]]（さくじんじゃ） *:甲府市に鎮座する式内論社。大宮山から天神山へ土本毘古王の古墳を改葬し、その跡に創建したとされる。 *[[金櫻神社]]（かなざくらじんじゃ） *:甲府市に鎮座する式内論社。景行朝に塩見宿禰に社殿の造営を命じて創祀されたと伝わる。 *[[金櫻神社 (山梨市)|金櫻神社]]（かなざくらじんじゃ） *:[[山梨市]]に鎮座する式内論社。京の丹波氏族である塩海足尼が本国の[[天橋立]]より橋立明神を勧請したと伝わる。
=== 墓 === *'''[[大丸山古墳]]'''（おおまるやまこふん） *:[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にある[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]中期の築造から塩海宿禰の父・臣知津彦命の墓か。 *'''[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]'''（かいちょうしづかこふん） *:山梨県甲府市にある前方後円墳で、4世紀後期の築造から国造初祖の塩海宿禰の墓か。 *'''[[天神山古墳 (甲府市)|天神山古墳]]'''（てんじんやまこふん） *:山梨県甲府市にある前方後円墳で、4世紀の築造から国造二代の速彦宿禰の墓か。
== 人物 == *'''[[塩海足尼]]'''（しおみのすくね、塩見足尼、塩海宿禰） *:[[景行天皇|景行朝]]の初代国造で、複数の神社の創建に関わった。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref> }}
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]]) * [[鈴木正信]]「甲斐国造の「氏姓」に関する再検討」（『日本史研究』584号、2011年） * 鈴木正信「甲斐国造の系譜に関する一考察」（『彦根論叢』391号、2012年）
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:甲斐国]] [[Category:甲斐国の氏族]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:山梨県の歴史]]

== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]]) * [[鈴木正信]]「甲斐国造の「氏姓」に関する再検討」（『日本史研究』584号、2011年） * 鈴木正信「甲斐国造の系譜に関する一考察」（『彦根論叢』391号、2012年）

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft|12=draft}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft|12=draft}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大倭国|12=draft|12=draft}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=draft|12=draft}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=draft|12=draft}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=draft|12=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft|12=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft|12=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=draft|12=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=draft|12=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
{{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}

__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
__TOC__{{-}}

=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|qq=}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Be system|qq=}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin|qq=}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki City|qq=}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu|qq=}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmaruyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大倭国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|qq=}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Be system|qq=}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin|qq=}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki City|qq=}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu|qq=}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmaruyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大倭国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}

This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu|qq=}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmaruyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=大倭国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|qq=}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Be system|qq=}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin|qq=}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki City|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki City|qq=}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu|qq=}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|ja|大丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmaruyama Kofun|qq=}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qq=}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|qq=}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}
[[sn:Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:甲斐国造]]

=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

[[sn:Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:甲斐国造]]
<!-- Crest image --><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--><!--Kai Kokuzo--> [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Yamanashi Prefecture]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}}.<ref>[[Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|qq=}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|qq=}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|qq=}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|qq=}}) }}

Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|qq=}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|qq=}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|qq=}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
