<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲斐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577872]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577872]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩海足尼}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|1=Mibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429506}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|1=Saigusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875188}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|1=Hokuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

(none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩海足尼}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|1=Mibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429506}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|1=Saigusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875188}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|1=Hokuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011.

clan|ja|三枝氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|1=Saigusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875188}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|1=Hokuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}, '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * [[:en:Masayuki Suzuki|Masayuki Suzuki]], "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011. {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]].
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.

The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]] during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]] and ink-marked pottery unearthed in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. ==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]].<ref name="houga2" />

== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)]], designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.

=== Burial Sites === - [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]]: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]]: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.

'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|WD=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * {{ill|Masayuki Suzuki|en|Masayuki Suzuki|WD=Q360719|lt=Masayuki Suzuki}}, "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011. {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:甲斐国造]]

== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|WD=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * {{ill|Masayuki Suzuki|en|Masayuki Suzuki|WD=Q360719|lt=Masayuki Suzuki}}, "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011. {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:甲斐国造]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩海足尼}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|1=Mibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429506}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|1=Saigusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875188}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|1=Hokuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>

Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|WD=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.

=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.

== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}

=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|WD=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * {{ill|Masayuki Suzuki|en|Masayuki Suzuki|WD=Q360719|lt=Masayuki Suzuki}}, "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011. {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:甲斐国造]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造) | crest = | crest_caption = | origin = {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | founder = {{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩海足尼}} | type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}) or {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} | homeland = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} | base = {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = {{ill|Mibu clan|ja|壬生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu clan|1=Mibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429506}}, {{ill|Saigusa clan|ja|三枝氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigusa clan|1=Saigusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24875188}} (samurai lineage)<ref>『三枝部氏初期綱要』</ref>, ({{ill|Hokuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni no Miyatsuko|1=Hokuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}, {{ill|Hamanakakenushi|ja|浜名県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanakakenushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) }}
'''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (甲斐国造, also read as Kai Koku-zō) was the title of the ruler governing {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}.
__TOC__{{-}}
== Overview == === Name === The title appears as '''Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} "Kuni no Miyatsuko Chronicles," and the {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|諸系譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[[:en:Makoto Suzuki|Makoto Suzuki]] "Eastern Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 1.</ref><ref name="nakata">[[Nakata Norinobu]] "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko" in *Shokeifu*, Vol. 7.</ref>
=== Ancestors === - According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the lineage traces back to Emperor Kaika, who married Princess {{ill|Oketsuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketsuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97496338}} of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417873}} lineage. Their son, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, married Princess {{ill|Saho no Ōkurami to Me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkurami to Me|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13360104}}, giving birth to {{ill|Saho no Hikoo|ja|沙本毘子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered the ancestor of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}. - The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} states that during Emperor Keikō’s reign, Shiomi no Sukune, a great-grandson of Saho no Hikoo, was appointed the first Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === The ruling clan, '''{{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}}''', bore the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. The claim of imperial descent has been a subject of scholarly debate (see [[#Analysis]]).
=== Analysis === ==== Clan and Kabane ==== The establishment of the Kuni no Miyatsuko system coincided with the rise of the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}} during the late 5th to 6th centuries, enabling the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}} to exert direct control over regional communities.
Artifacts such as the ink-inscribed textiles from {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|WD=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin}} and ink-marked pottery unearthed in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki City}} confirm the presence of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in Kai Province. Scholars like [[Akira Seki]] identify the Kusakabe as likely sharing the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason}} or {{ill|Na|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, which aligns with local governance roles.
==== Archaeological Links ==== The construction of {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} in the 4th century indicates strong ties to the Yamato royal court. This connection is further substantiated by the discovery of {{ill|Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakubuchi Shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} in the area.
== Base of Power == Shiomi no Sukune and subsequent rulers were centered in the northern part of Kai Province, particularly in the area extending from present-day Fuefuki to eastern {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}.<ref name="houga2" />
=== Domain === The Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko likely governed the entirety of Kai Province, as no evidence suggests the presence of additional Kuni no Miyatsuko in the region.
== Clan Shrines == The clan's primary shrine was the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}}, designated as the Ichinomiya of Kai Province.
=== Related Shrines === - {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}}: Located in Kōfu, associated with the second ruler, Hayahiko no Sukune. - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}}: Founded by Shiomi no Sukune under Emperor Keikō's orders. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}: Associated with the worship of Yamato Takeru.
=== Burial Sites === - {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}: Believed to be the burial site of Chitohiko no Mikoto. - {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}: Possibly the tomb of Shiomi no Sukune.
== Notable Figures == * '''{{ill|Shiomi no Sukune|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}''': The first ruler, credited with founding several shrines and establishing Kai Province's governance.
== References == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="houga2">[[Hoga Toshio]]. "Genealogy and History of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko," *Kojukyo no Fūken*, 2016.</ref> }}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Sources == * *Kokushi Daijiten* (吉川弘文館) * Sakamoto Taro and Hirano Kunio, *Japanese Ancient Clan Dictionary* (吉川弘文館) * *Nihonshi Kōjiten* (山川出版社) * *Shintō Daijiten* (臨川書店) * {{ill|Masayuki Suzuki|en|Masayuki Suzuki|WD=Q360719|lt=Masayuki Suzuki}}, "Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko and Governance," *Journal of Japanese History*, 2011. {{translated page|ja|甲斐国造|version=95211027|comment=Imported full ja history}}

__TOC__{{-}}
